decks.change_notes: Muxus from Jumpstart was the last major addition. Struggles against Bowmasters.

key_cards_explained.Goblin Lackey: Deals combat damage to put any goblin from hand into play free.
key_cards_explained.Muxus, Goblin Grandee: Reveals top 6 and puts all goblins MV 5 or less into play.
key_cards_explained.Goblin Ringleader: Reveals top 4 and puts all goblins into hand.

historical_timeline.event: Goblins is tier 1. Lackey and Warchief enable incredibly fast starts.
Still competitive but losing ground to blue tempo.
Muxus from Jumpstart is a massive addition.
Bowmasters kills most goblins and devastates the strategy.
Fringe at 0.4%. Loyal pilots only.

decks.name: Nic Fit
decks.description: A ramp midrange deck using Veteran Explorer sacrificed to Cabal Therapy to ramp, then plays expensive threats opponents can't match.
decks.similarity_notes: Shares BG midrange core with Maverick but goes much bigger with Veteran Explorer ramp.
decks.change_notes: Veteran Explorer + Cabal Therapy is the unchanging core. Splash colors vary.

key_cards_explained.Veteran Explorer: When it dies, each player searches for two basic lands. Most Legacy decks play 0-2 basics.
key_cards_explained.Cabal Therapy: Names a card to strip from opponent's hand. Sac Explorer to flashback.
key_cards_explained.Pernicious Deed: Destroys all nonland permanents CMC X or less.
key_cards_explained.Phyrexian Tower: Sacs creatures for BB. Sacs Explorer at instant speed.

historical_timeline.event: Nic Fit emerges exploiting Legacy's low basic land counts.
Peak popularity. Siege Rhino makes Abzan preferred.
Bowmasters and power creep push to fringes.
Fringe at 0.2%. Beloved pet deck.

decks.name: Food Chain
decks.description: A combo deck using Food Chain with Misthollow Griffin or Eternal Scourge to generate infinite creature mana for Walking Ballista.
decks.similarity_notes: Like Aluren, wins with an enchantment-based creature combo. Aluren is more competitive currently.
decks.change_notes: Core combo is fixed. Manipulate Fate exiles Griffins/Scourges for the combo.

key_cards_explained.Food Chain: Exile a creature for CMC+1 mana, creature spells only. Infinite with exile-cast creatures.
key_cards_explained.Misthollow Griffin: Cast from exile. Nets 1 mana each loop with Food Chain.
key_cards_explained.Manipulate Fate: Exiles 3 cards from library, draws a card. Sets up exile-cast creatures.
key_cards_explained.Walking Ballista: Infinite mana outlet. Cast for X=infinite.

historical_timeline.event: Food Chain + Misthollow Griffin combo refined for Legacy.
Peak popularity. Walking Ballista as clean win condition.
Power creep pushes deck down. Combo too slow.
Fringe at 0.2%. Pet deck for combo enthusiasts.

decks.name: Enchantress
decks.description: A prison deck using Argothian Enchantress and Enchantress's Presence to draw cards off enchantments, locking opponents out with Solitary Confinement and Elephant Grass.
decks.similarity_notes: Like Lands, a non-blue prison deck aiming to lock the opponent out.
decks.change_notes: Sythis from MH2 was a huge upgrade. Otherwise very stable.

key_cards_explained.Argothian Enchantress: Draws a card for each enchantment cast.
key_cards_explained.Solitary Confinement: Can't be targeted and take no damage. Enchantress draws fuel the upkeep discard.
key_cards_explained.Sterling Grove: Gives enchantments shroud and sacs to tutor.
key_cards_explained.Elephant Grass: Opponents must pay mana to attack.

historical_timeline.event: Argothian Enchantress from Urza's Saga enables enchantment strategies.
Viable Legacy deck with Solitary Confinement lock.
Sythis from MH2 adds a third efficient enchantress.
Fringe at 0.2%. Niche prison deck beloved by loyal pilots.

decks.name: High Tide
decks.description: A mono-blue combo deck using High Tide to double Island mana, then untapping with Time Spiral and Turnabout to generate massive mana and win with Blue Sun's Zenith or Brain Freeze.
decks.similarity_notes: Fellow blue combo deck aiming to win in one turn. Requires careful sequencing.
decks.change_notes: Historic deck. Last competitively relevant around 2015. Format too hostile for slow mono-blue combo.

key_cards_explained.High Tide: Islands produce an additional U until end of turn.
key_cards_explained.Time Spiral: Untaps up to six lands, each player draws seven. Nets massive mana with High Tide.
key_cards_explained.Candelabra of Tawnos: Taps for X to untap X lands. Doubles mana with High Tide.

historical_bans_impact.impact: Banned early in Legacy. Would have been another untap effect.

historical_timeline.event: High Tide from Fallen Empires combined with untap effects creates early combo.
Peak viability. Specialists like Feline Longmore put up results.
Format too fast. High Tide too slow and fragile.
Effectively unplayable in Bowmasters era.

decks.name: Belcher
decks.description: An all-in combo deck playing zero or one lands, using fast mana to activate Goblin Charbelcher for lethal damage when no lands remain in library.
decks.similarity_notes: Like Oops, wins with almost no lands. Both fold to Force of Will but are blazing fast.
decks.change_notes: Very stable. Chancellor of the Tangle and Irencrag Feat were recent additions.

key_cards_explained.Goblin Charbelcher: Activate for 3 to reveal until a land, dealing that much damage. With 0-1 lands, lethal.
key_cards_explained.Lion's Eye Diamond: Discard hand for 3 mana. With Burning Wish, cast Wish first then crack LED.
key_cards_explained.Land Grant: Reveals hand to search for a Forest (Taiga). Free spell.

historical_timeline.event: Charbelcher combo discovered for Legacy.
Consistent fringe combo. Folds to Force of Will.
Chancellor of the Tangle provides free mana.
Fringe at 0.1%. Ultra-budget combo.

decks.name: Stiflenought
decks.description: A tempo deck using Stifle to counter Phyrexian Dreadnought's sacrifice trigger, creating a 12/12 trampler for two mana total.
decks.similarity_notes: Essentially Dimir Tempo with Stifle/Dreadnought instead of Tamiyo and Barrowgoyf. Trades consistency for explosive potential.
decks.change_notes: Dress Down from MH2 provides an additional way to negate the trigger.

key_cards_explained.Phyrexian Dreadnought: 12/12 trampler for 1 with sacrifice trigger. Stifle negates it.
key_cards_explained.Stifle: Counters triggered ability for U. Also hits fetchlands.
key_cards_explained.Dress Down: Removes all abilities. Dreadnought enters as vanilla 12/12.
key_cards_explained.Wasteland: Compounds the mana denial angle with Stifle on fetchlands.

historical_timeline.event: Stifle + Dreadnought becomes a Legacy staple interaction.
Peak popularity in various tempo shells.
Dress Down from MH2 as backup enabler. Brief renewed interest.
Fringe at 0.1%. Dimir Tempo is strictly better.

decks.name: Merfolk
decks.description: A tribal aggro deck using lord effects for unblockable islandwalk damage. Combines Aether Vial with Force of Will for aggro-control.
decks.similarity_notes: Tribal Aether Vial deck like Goblins. Uniquely has Force of Will access among tribal decks.
decks.change_notes: Vodalian Hexcatcher from Dominaria United was a major addition. Svyelun from MH2 added card advantage.

key_cards_explained.Lord of Atlantis: All merfolk get +1/+1 and islandwalk. Unblockable vs blue decks.
key_cards_explained.True-Name Nemesis: Protection from a chosen player. Untargetable, unblockable.
key_cards_explained.Vodalian Hexcatcher: Lord with flash that sacs merfolk to counter noncreature spells.
key_cards_explained.Aether Vial: Cheats merfolk in at instant speed.

historical_timeline.event: Merfolk tribal becomes competitive with critical mass of lords.
True-Name Nemesis is a massive upgrade.
Vodalian Hexcatcher adds a counterspell lord.
Bowmasters rough for small creatures.
Fringe at 0.2%. Loyal tribe with FoW edge.
key_changes: Force of Will gives unique edge

decks.name: MUD
decks.description: A colorless artifact stompy deck using Metalworker and Ancient Tomb to power out threats, backed by Chalice and Trinisphere.
decks.similarity_notes: Shares Chalice + fast mana shell with Eldrazi and Red Stompy. More artifact-focused with Metalworker.
decks.change_notes: Karn wishboard and Urza's Saga are the main recent innovations.

key_cards_explained.Metalworker: Taps to reveal artifacts from hand, adding 2 colorless each.
key_cards_explained.Chalice of the Void: On one, counters most of Legacy's best spells.
key_cards_explained.Karn, the Great Creator: Shuts off artifacts and wishes for silver bullets.
key_cards_explained.Trinisphere: All spells cost at least 3. Devastating against cantrip decks.

historical_timeline.event: MUD emerges using Metalworker and artifact mana.
Peak popularity. Walking Ballista as great mana sink.
Karn wishboard transforms the deck.
Tier 3 at 0.5%. Competes with other stompy variants.

decks.name: Ninjas
decks.description: A tempo deck using evasive creatures to enable ninjutsu, primarily Yuriko. Yuriko's trigger drains based on revealed card's mana value.
decks.similarity_notes: Shares UB tempo shell with Dimir Tempo but replaces threats with ninja package.
decks.change_notes: Moon-Circuit Hacker from Kamigawa was a solid addition. Core ninja package stable.

key_cards_explained.Yuriko, the Tiger's Shadow: Commander ninjutsu for UB bypasses command tax. Drains on combat damage.
key_cards_explained.Changeling Outcast: Unblockable 1/1 for B. Perfect ninjutsu enabler.
key_cards_explained.Ingenious Infiltrator: Draws a card whenever any ninja deals combat damage.
key_cards_explained.Brainstorm: Puts high-MV cards on top for Yuriko to reveal.

historical_timeline.event: Yuriko from Commander 2018 creates a new archetype.
Peak popularity with Ingenious Infiltrator.
Bowmasters kills enabler creatures.
Fringe at 0.2%. Fun alternative to Dimir Tempo.

decks.name: Esper Vial
decks.description: A creature toolbox deck using Aether Vial, Stoneforge Mystic, and Recruiter of the Guard to assemble the right creatures for each matchup.
decks.similarity_notes: A DnT variant adding blue and black for more interaction. Stoneblade is the spell-oriented version.
decks.change_notes: Bowmasters was a huge addition. Recruiter chains for specific answers.

key_cards_explained.Aether Vial: Cheats creatures in at instant speed for free.
key_cards_explained.Recruiter of the Guard: Tutors for any creature with toughness 2 or less.
key_cards_explained.Stoneforge Mystic: Tutors equipment and cheats it into play.

historical_timeline.event: Recruiter of the Guard from Conspiracy 2 enables Esper toolbox.
Peak popularity with diverse toolbox.
Bowmasters joins but also punishes small creatures.
Fringe at 0.2%. DnT generally preferred.

decks.name: Jund
decks.description: A three-color midrange deck combining the best discard, removal, and threats in BRG. Barrowgoyf, Thoughtseize, Lightning Bolt alongside green utility.
decks.similarity_notes: Shares BG disruption with Nic Fit but without ramp. Mardu Energy is the preferred non-blue disruption aggro deck.
decks.change_notes: Barrowgoyf from MH3 replaced Tarmogoyf. Without Brainstorm, lacks card selection.

key_cards_explained.Barrowgoyf: MH3 Tarmogoyf upgrade. Efficient threat with diverse graveyard types.
key_cards_explained.Lightning Bolt: Efficient removal that also goes to face.
key_cards_explained.Abrupt Decay: Uncounterable removal for CMC 3 or less.

historical_bans_impact.impact: Lost its best one-drop mana fixer. DRS held three-color bases together.

historical_timeline.event: Jund midrange appears in Legacy.
DRS makes three-color mana consistent.
DRS ban devastates the mana base.
Barrowgoyf from MH3 and Bowmasters bring some interest.
Fringe at 0.2%. Outclassed by blue decks.

decks.name: Bomberman
decks.description: A combo deck using Auriok Salvagers with Lion's Eye Diamond to generate infinite mana, fueling lethal Pyrite Spellbomb or Walking Ballista.
decks.similarity_notes: Like Painter, an artifact combo in a fair shell. Mystic Forge uses similar artifact mana.
decks.change_notes: Historic deck last seen competitively around 2015. Combo too slow for modern Legacy.

key_cards_explained.Auriok Salvagers: Returns artifact MV 1 or less from graveyard for 1W. Infinite loop with LED.
key_cards_explained.Lion's Eye Diamond: Discard hand for 3 mana. With Salvagers, discard is irrelevant.
key_cards_explained.Pyrite Spellbomb: 2 damage for R + sac. Recur infinitely for lethal.
key_cards_explained.Enlightened Tutor: Finds LED or the kill condition.

historical_timeline.event: Salvagers + LED combo discovered.
Peak viability with Stoneforge fair backup.
Power creep makes combo too slow.
Effectively historic. Painter is better in every way.

decks.name: Hive Mind
decks.description: A combo deck using Show and Tell for Hive Mind, then casting Pacts that the opponent copies but cannot pay for on their upkeep.
decks.similarity_notes: Shares Show and Tell shell with Sneak and Show but uses Hive Mind + Pacts instead of creatures.
decks.change_notes: Historic deck. Alternative to Sneak and Show that won through creature-hosing effects. Format outgrew it.

key_cards_explained.Hive Mind: Copies every instant/sorcery for all players. Pacts force unplayable upkeep costs.
key_cards_explained.Summoner's Pact: Free spell. Opponent's copy requires 2GG next upkeep or lose.
key_cards_explained.Show and Tell: Each player puts a permanent into play. You put Hive Mind.
key_cards_explained.Pact of Negation: Free counter with 3UU upkeep. Opponent can't pay.

historical_timeline.event: Hive Mind combo discovered using Show and Tell and Pacts.
Peak popularity as alternative to Sneak and Show.
Sneak and Show proves more resilient. Hive Mind fades.
Effectively historic. Omni-Tell is better.

decks.name: UW Miracles
decks.description: The post-Sensei's Divining Top version of Miracles. Uses Terminus and Entreat the Angels set up with Brainstorm and Jace rather than the banned Top.
decks.similarity_notes: Predecessor to Jeskai Control. Jeskai adds red for Pyroblast and Phlage. Beanstalk is more popular currently.
decks.change_notes: After Top ban in 2017, rebuilt around Brainstorm. Counterbalance without Top is less consistent. Predict helps set up miracles.

key_cards_explained.Terminus: One-mana miracle that tucks all creatures. Set up with Brainstorm.
key_cards_explained.Counterbalance: Reveals top card to counter matching MV spells. Without Top, relies on cantrips.
key_cards_explained.Jace, the Mind Sculptor: Brainstorms every turn, bounces, threatens ultimate.

historical_bans_impact.impact: Original Miracles with Top was the best deck for years. Top ban forced complete rebuild.

historical_timeline.event: Original Miracles with Top dominates. Counterbalance + Top is near-unbreakable.
Top banned April 2017. Players rebuild with Brainstorm as miracle enabler.
Post-Top Miracles competitive but not dominant. Narset and planeswalkers strengthen shell.
Bowmasters weakens Jace and Brainstorm setup. Jeskai Control preferred.
Tier 3 at 0.5%. Nostalgic but outclassed by Jeskai and Beanstalk Control.
